content.problemStatement:
Rectify the errors in the program to get the desired output

Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer representing the user's choice. The integer can be any value in the range -10^9 … 10^9.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output exactly one line:
- "Option 1 selected" if the choice equals 1
- "Option 2 selected" if the choice equals 2
- "Option 3 selected" if the choice equals 3
- "Invalid choice" for any other integer

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case; the choice 1 maps to the first option.
Single test case, choice 2 selects the second option.
Choice 5 is not among 1, 2, 3, so the program prints "Invalid choice".

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 3 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 1 selected
Invalid choice
Invalid choice

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. Loop T times, reading the integer choice each iteration. Use a series of if‑else statements to compare choice with 1, 2 and 3 and print the corresponding message; otherwise print "Invalid choice". The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space (apart from the output).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used; output storage is not counted.
